title: quadlet: No way to pass empty string ('') as Entrypoint
body:
### Issue Description

When using quadlet to run a containerized service, it does not seem to be possible to use the `Entrypoint=` option to set the entry point to nothing.  AFAICT, the only way to do this is with `PodmanArgs=--entrypoint=`.  

This seems like something that should be doable with `Entrypoint=`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Try setting `Entrypoint=` or `Entrypoint=""`.  Neither of these add any `--entrypoint` option to the generated `podman` command.  (Verified with `sudo /usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator --dryrun`.)

`Entrypoint="''"` or Entrypoint='""'` both pass doubly quoted or escaped options, which don't work.


### Describe the results you received

See above.

### Describe the results you expected

There should be a way to set the `--entrypoint` option of the generated `podman` command to nothing (or an empty string).

### podman info output
